title: 2020_Amazon Shopping_Ad Fraud_CONFIDENTIAL_case study_FINAL
excerpt: We areWe connect your Brand To today’s connected consumers Amazon Shopping is the largest online retailer in the United States. We have been working with Amazon Shopping in the USA since 2011. Headquartered in Seattle, the retailer is the most popular online marketplace used by both individual as well as businesses In 2021, Amazon surpassed Walmartas the world's largest retailer outside ofChina. Growth was driven in large part by its paid subscription plan,Amazon Prime, which has over 200million subscribers globally. Protecting against ad fraud,amazon shopping Page 3 Our goal was to generate a high volume of downloads through in-app display & decrease ad fraud levels. The Objective: The challenge:fraudulent installsWorking with ad networks allowed us to drive installs at a guaranteed CPI, however the lack of transparency within the buying model made Amazon highly susceptible to fraud. A different approach was required to prevent ad fraud from ad networks. our APPROACH: •We partnered with a 3rdparty fraud monitoring solution to implement numerous new fraud prevention rules.•Activity was optimized at the placement, source, and ad network levelto reduce fraud rates and drive more high-quality installs.•Fraud makegoods were implemented each month, saving Amazonmillions in ad budget. campaignapproach Page 4 We analysed trusted display channels to determine normal traffic trends and how much variation is to be expected. Results that were significantly outside the norm (iex2 standard variations) were marked as fraud and deducted from billing. Ad networks received fraud reports daily to track pacing and make optimizations.Sources that showed high fraud rates were blacklisted on a weekly cadence, with clean sources scaling up with additional budget. Ad Networks that had high fraud overall (>30%) were removed from media plans for the subsequent month. Recognizing the broader economic climate and consumer sensitivity around spending, Rakuten also experimented with new creative angles that resonated more deeply with its audience. For example, we launched and tested a campaign around “Essentials”, highlighting how Rakuten helps shoppers save on everyday necessities. This approach tapped into consumer sentiment and reinforced the brand’s value proposition in a relatable, timely way.

title: 2025_USA_Rakuten_TikTok Spark+_Case Study
excerpt: Case Study Driving Campaign Efficiency on TikTok with Smart+ for Rakuten About Rakuten Rakuten is a leading shopping and rewards platform that offers Cash Back from over 3,500 partner stores across categories such as fashion, beauty, and more. Challenge Rising Cost Pressures Despite continuous creative testing, costs remained stubbornly high. This left Rakuten with cost-per metrics above target and highlighted the need for smarter delivery and more advanced optimization tactics. Our Approach Rakuten’s strategy combined consistency in creative with innovation in media execution. No major shift was made in the creative direction; instead, the emphasis was on leveraging existing content to power the campaigns. From a media perspective, we tested two distinct Smart+ campaigns, one built entirely with Spark Ads and another solely with TikTok One videos. Each campaign was allocated a modest budget of $10K, giving the algorithm space to automatically optimize creative delivery and media placement in real time.
